01_initial_request.input_data: Literature records from PubMed, Science Direct, Scopus, Cochrane Library and EMBASE databases published until July 2020
01_initial_request.user_intent: To assess whether the subnasal lip lift technique improves lip aesthetics and maintains its stability in adult patients
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Improvement in lip aesthetics based on anthropometric measures and patient satisfaction, and stability duration ranging from 12 to 59.1 months

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Lip lift procedures exist but lack standardization of techniques and long-term stability data. [Gap] There is no consolidated evidence on whether subnasal lip lift improves aesthetics and maintains stability in adult patients. [Decision] To achieve a comprehensive assessment, chose to use Electronic Database Search to perform a systematic literature search on PubMed, Science Direct, Scopus, Cochrane Library and EMBASE, expecting to obtain relevant literature records published until July 2020.

parameters.search_terms: "lip lift" OR "Lip lifting" AND "subnasal lift" OR "subnasal lifting"

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 464 articles initially found (33 in PubMed, 42 in Scopus, 158 in Science Direct, 0 in Cochrane, 231 in EMBASE).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 464 articles were identified from the database search. [Gap] Need to filter these articles to identify eligible studies based on inclusion criteria (cohorts, case series) and exclude those with insufficient information. [Decision] To achieve study selection, chose to use RYYAN ® program to perform screening of titles, abstracts, and full texts on the 464 articles, expecting to obtain a final set of eligible studies.

parameters.inclusion_criteria: Retrospective or prospective cohort studies, case control and case series
parameters.screening_process: Titles and abstracts analysis, followed by full text evaluation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 4 studies selected (2 retrospective cohorts and 2 case series) after removing duplicates and excluding 44 full texts.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 4 studies were selected for inclusion. [Gap] Need to assess the quality and risk of bias of the included studies to ensure reliability of the review. [Decision] To achieve quality assurance, chose to use Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions to perform quality assessment on the 4 included studies, expecting to obtain a quality rating for each study.

tool.name: Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Quality assessed for 4 studies (2 retrospective cohorts and 2 case series).

references.citation: Higgins J, Thomas J, editors. Cochrane handbook for systematic reviews of interventions [Internet]. Version 6, 2019
references.relevance: Provides the methodology and standards for assessing the quality of the included studies in the systematic review.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 4 quality-assessed studies are available. [Gap] Need to extract specific data on lip aesthetics, patient satisfaction, and stability to answer the research question. [Decision] To achieve data synthesis, chose to use Data Extraction Protocol to perform extraction of anthropometric measures, satisfaction scores, and follow-up times on the 4 included studies, expecting to obtain quantitative and qualitative outcomes.

parameters.data_points: Amount of tissue removed, exposure of labial vermilion, score of upper lip/lower face, labial score/filter, patient satisfaction, follow-up time
parameters.analysis_method: Descriptive analysis (Meta-analysis prevented due to methodology differences)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 92.4% of cases were women (mean age 36.6 years). 92.1%-100% patient satisfaction. Stability maintained from 12 to 59.1 months. Improvement in lip aesthetics confirmed.

references.citation: Holden PK, Sufyan AS, Perkins SW (2011); Jung JA, et al. (2019); Lee DE, et al. (2015); Pan BL (2017)
references.relevance: These are the 4 included studies from which data was extracted and analyzed.

patient_satisfaction.interpretation: High degree of patient satisfaction reported across studies.

stability_duration.interpretation: Results maintained for varying periods with low revision rate.

aesthetic_improvement.interpretation: Confirmed by anthropometric measures (vermilion exposure, lip height, nasolabial angle).

demographic_prevalence.interpretation: Percentage of cases performed in women.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The subnasal lip lift improves lip aesthetics and maintains its stability for a certain period of time (approximately 28 months average) in adult patients, though standardization of techniques is still needed.
